revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a game-development pixel-art asset sheet.

Canvas and grid:
- Exact final image size: 64x64 pixels.
- 4 rows x 4 columns, 16 total cells.
- Each cell is exactly 16x16 pixels.
- Do not draw visible grid lines, borders, tile IDs, labels, UI, text, watermark, or signatures.
- Every decoration must stay fully inside its own 16x16 cell, centered or near-centered, with consistent scale.

Style/profile:
- SNES / 16-bit platformer-world-map pixel art, crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur.
- Bright mythical-Japan palette, cheerful and readable.
- Top-left light source.
- One-pixel dark outline where useful.
- Consistent palette and identity across all 16 frames.

Background:
- Fill all empty space in every cell with solid opaque #FF00FF magenta.
- This is a sparse decorative OVERLAY tile set, so each cell should be mostly empty magenta with exactly one small Japanese-countryside decoration near the center.

Subject: sparse decorative overlay tiles for deep-green Japanese sasa-grass ground, top-down, world-map scale. Use pointed slender grass and natural countryside detail, not close-up village lawn texture. Each of the 16 cells should contain one small unique decoration variant, such as:
1. tiny tuft of sharp pointed sasa bamboo-grass blades
2. slender clump of pointed deep-green grass
3. soft green moss on a small grey stone
4. sprig of violet tsuyukusa dayflowers
5. little pink nadeshiko flower cluster
6. a few fallen pale-pink cherry petals
7. couple of smooth river pebbles
8. tiny white wood-sorrel cluster
9. chipped sasa blade tuft with lighter tips
10. small worn patch with sparse grass blades
11. tiny mossy pebble pair
12. single violet dayflower with leaf sprig
13. loose pink petals in a small arc
14. thin grass-overlap sprout cluster
15. small grey stone with soft green moss highlight
16. very sparse mixed countryside sprig with pointed leaves

Tilemap quality:
- Keep every cell sparse and usable as an overlay decoration.
- Avoid repeated square stamps, hard rectangular bands, or checkerboard texture.
- Vary silhouette, tiny lighting clusters, and edge breakup while preserving cohesive scale.
- Think world-map material: delicate regional patch details and low-frequency texture, not dense close-up ground.

Strict negatives:
- NOT round clover, NOT dandelions.
- No Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, references, or likenesses.
- No samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, guns, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, birds, companion creatures, letter emblems, modern text, watermark, UI.
- No visible grid lines or borders.

Output must be crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with readable pixel clusters, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, and one small decoration per 16x16 cell.
